core_mechanism: q-exponential (Tsallis nonextensive-statistics) decay law — a two-parameter deformation of exponential decay whose deformation parameter q continuously interpolates between memoryless exponential relaxation and power-law/hyperbolic decay, separating the overall decay rate from the deviation-from-pure-exponential
brief_reason: The abstract parametrizes the decay of value over time with 'the q-exponential discount function based on Tsallis' statistics,' whose parameters distinguish the decay rate (impulsivity) from the departure from time-consistent pure-exponential decay (inconsistency).
